headline: Risk-off Friday open: NFLX -11% on Q3 guide miss + memory chip panic (SNDK/WDC/MU/STX -3-5%) + ISRG -10% on recall drive NQ -1.67% hard, WTI +2.2% to $80.60 on Iran oil-license revocation keeps XLE bid — health/financials defensive bid, 10Y 4.58% holds tape fragile into UMich sentiment.

sentiment.reason: NQ -1.67% leads ES -0.79% / YM -0.52% lower as NFLX Q3 guide miss re-ignites AI-valuation jitters (NFLX -20% YTD, -40% from ATH) on top of 3rd-day memory chip panic + ISRG -10% recall; WTI +2.2% to $80.60 (Iran license revocations) + 10Y at 4.58% cap relief; only offsets are XLV/XLE defensive bid and YM resilience.

movers.reason: Q3 rev guide of +11.7% YoY = lowest growth since late-2023; FY guide narrowed to $51-51.4B; engagement-metric concerns + $80 EPS in-line but revenue $12.56B slight miss; YTD -20%, -40% from ATH.
Q2 beat ($2.89B rev +2.6% / EPS $2.80 +11.9%) but Class II recall on da Vinci components + softening surgical volumes at major medical institutions; binary tape ignores the print.
3rd-day memory complex panic; SNDK -2.6% / WDC -4%+ / STX -3%+ all confirming; WDC leading the downside as NAND-cycle fears persist post-Samsung.

news.headline: NFLX -11% premarket: Q3 rev guide +11.7% YoY = lowest growth since late-2023; FY narrows to $51-51.4B
news.context: Engagement-metric concerns re-ignite AI-valuation jitters across mega-cap tech; NFLX YTD -20%, -40% off ATH. NQ -1.67% lead driver. Re-validates the tech concentration risk tape — XLK now 20%+ of SPX.

news.headline: US revokes Iranian oil-sales licenses effective July 17 — WTI +2.2% to $80.60, Brent +1.9% to $85.89
news.context: WTI +12% on the week as Hormuz/Red Sea closure risk returns; XLE bid persists, but stagflation tilt keeps 10Y sticky at 4.58% and caps relief-rally window. Tape = oil-immune bid, growth-headwind.

news.headline: Memory chip complex panic day 3: SNDK -2.6%, WDC -4%+, STX -3%+, MU -3%
news.context: AI-memory trade unwinds for a 3rd session post-Samsung; optical/comm (GLW -4%, LITE -3%, MRVL -3%) extends lead-down. KC/Kospi closed Thu to avoid storm; reopens Mon face catch-down pressure. Cycle-peak narrative taking hold.

news.headline: ISRG -9.8% despite Q2 beat: Class II recall on da Vinci + softening surgical volumes
news.context: Hospitals reporting volume declines; managed-care re-rate bid into XLV (+0.85%) but single-name tape binary — ISRG breaks trend with the recall. Healthcare defensives still bid via UNH/managed-care, but med-tech under pressure.

news.headline: June IP 9:15 ET, July UMich sentiment 10:00 ET — Friday data dump into 3-day weekend
news.context: UMich sentiment the closer; soft print = relief, hot = stagflation. WTI $80.60 + 10Y 4.58% = tape fragile into both. Vol being sold into the long weekend with VIX 16.7 — another fragile calm signal.

options.thesis: NQ -1.67% with VIX only 16.7 = front-week IV mispriced vs realized; memory/NFLX selloff drives gamma flip negative below 28,800. Dealer gamma long above 29,200 but pressured.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (28,800/28,500) into UMich print; defined-risk into 3-day weekend. VIX >18.5 = take profit.

options.thesis: ES -0.79% with XLV +0.85% (UNH) + XLE +1.10% rotating defensive — tape is bifurcating, not breaking. 10Y 4.58% caps duration relief. COR3M still compressed at ~9.5.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,450/7,350 put spreads July 24, fund with short 7,650 calls = risk-reversal for the long-weekend/IP-tail.

options.thesis: Disconnected from semis selloff + oil tape; IV 55% with no fresh catalyst into Q2 deliveries next week. Premium sellers favored as TSLA -1% lags the AI/NFLX drag.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~280) into Friday close; vol crush favors sellers over 3-day weekend. No directional edge into macro binary.

options.thesis: Surprise pick — NFLX -11% pre-mkt with IV expanding 90%+ as Q3 guide disappoints; dealer flow two-way. 30D vol has 48-72h shelf life as engagement fears re-price the streaming-multiple thesis.
options.signal: Long NFLX Aug 1,200/1,000 put spread (~$25) into Friday open as single-name tail; defined-risk into long weekend. Pair with short Aug 800 puts to finance if IV rich.

options.thesis: Surprise pick — WDC -4% premkt, 3rd-day memory panic; IV 80%+ expanding, unusual put flow in front-week 65Ps. NAND-cycle peak narrative is the dominant setup; supply-side validation from SK Hynix capacity ramp not sticking.
options.signal: Long WDC Aug 60/45 put spread (~$2.50); single-name beta to memory-trade unwind, defined-risk into Mon reopen.

takeaway: Risk-off Friday: NFLX -11% Q3 guide + memory chip day-3 panic + ISRG -10% recall stack = NQ -1.67% lead-down, ES -0.79%, YM -0.52%. WTI +2.2% to $80.60 (Iran license revocation) keeps XLE bid and 10Y sticky at 4.58% — stagflation tilt cap. Only offsets are XLV/XLE defensive bid. Trade: long QQQ/SPY put spreads into UMich + long weekend, sell TSLA vol, ride NFLX/WDC single-name puts into Mon reopen. Tape fragile into 3-day weekend, VIX 16.7 is too cheap.

headline: Soft inflation + jobless claims bid (bullish) vs Iran 2nd-wave strikes + chip complex sell-the-news (bearish): split tape, NQ -0.83% leads ES -0.27% lower while Dow flat; ATAI +55% on Lilly M&A, UNH +7% on raised 2026 guide, but semis bleed (TSMC -3.9% post-beat, WDC -7.2%, STX -5.7%, MU -5%) + UAL -3.4% on oil — WTI $79.54 holding the bid on Hormuz/Houthi tail risk into Netflix AMC and 17K-bank earnings wave.

sentiment.reason: Macro tailwind (retail +0.2% but cooling, jobless claims 208K, June PPI/CPI benign) offset by chip sell-the-news after TSMC beat + Iran 2nd-wave strikes keeping WTI/UST 10Y bid; NQ -0.83% vs YM +0.10% = clear split between long-duration and value/health complex.

movers.reason: Bloomberg: Eli Lilly in advanced M&A talks, formal takeover could land this week; VC sees up to 105% takeover premium on BPL-003 psychedelic pipeline.
Q2 earnings beat, raised 2026 adj-EPS to $19.50-20.00 vs $18.49 Street; Humana +4.4% / Centene +3.5% lifted by managed-care re-rate.
Memory complex in profit-taking mode after SK Hynix post-IPO surge; STX -5.7% / MU -5% / SNDK -7%+ confirming cap-ex digestion trade.

news.headline: Trump says Iran wants to negotiate even as US launches 2nd-wave strikes; Iran asks Houthis to ready Red Sea closure
news.context: WTI holds $79.54 on Hormuz/Houthi tail-risk repricing; defies softer CPI/claims. Risk-asset drag persists, defense/energy bid intact.

news.headline: TSMC +77% Q2 profit beat, raises FY capex to $64B + plans extra $100B Arizona — US-listed shares drop 3.9% premkt
news.context: Sell-the-news on cap-ex digestion vs SK Hynix post-IPO unwind; semis split-tape widens (NVDA/AVGO bid while MU/STX/WDC bleed).

news.headline: Eli Lilly (LLY) in advanced talks to acquire AtaiBeckley (ATAI); ATAI +55%, deal possible this week
news.context: Healthcare M&A tape ignites (UNH, HUM, CNC lift); XLV +1.4% pre-mkt. Confirms biotech big-pharma re-rate as 2026 theme.

news.headline: Jobless claims drop to 208K (vs 217K est); June retail sales +0.2%; Philly Fed surges to 41.4 (5-yr high)
news.context: Soft-landing data trifecta: labor cooling, consumer still spending, manufacturing rebound. 88-89% odds Fed on hold at July meeting; supports ES bid into afternoon.

news.headline: Netflix reports AMC; UNH +6.8% lifts managed care; UAL -3.4% on oil; GE -2.9% despite guidance raise
news.context: Earnings tape bifurcating: managed care / industrials with oil-immune margins bid; airlines bleed on WTI $79. NFLX is the single-name binary into close.

options.thesis: NQ -0.83% with VIX only 16.2 = vol being sold into a binary tape (Iran 2nd wave + chip digestion + Netflix AMC). Dealer gamma flipping negative below 29,500; realized vol is decoupling from IV.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (29,500/29,200) into Netflix AMC; size defined-risk. VIX >17.5 = take profit. Cleanest hedge into the day's binary stack.

options.thesis: ES -0.27% with XLV +1.4% (UNH-led) and XLF +0.18% offsetting semis/tech drag = tape is rotating, not breaking. 10Y at 4.57% (off Mon 4.59% high) = real-yield pressure easing, but Iran tail still live.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,550/7,450 put spreads (Jul 17) funded by short 7,650 calls = risk-reversal for Iran/NFLX tail; cheap convexity into the close.

options.thesis: Disconnected from semis selloff + oil tape; IV 52% with no fresh catalyst into Q2 deliveries next week. Premium sellers favored; dealers still leaning long gamma above 290.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~290) into Netflix/close; vol crush favors sellers. No directional edge into macro binary — pure premium play.

options.thesis: Surprise pick — Bloomberg confirms LLY M&A in advanced stage, formal announcement possible this week. Stock +55% already, but options market still pricing a 60-70% deal-completion probability. Risk-reversal skewed cheap.
options.signal: Long ATAI Aug 12 calls (~$2.10) as Marbeck deal-completion hedge; size small, defined-risk. Pair with short Aug 18 calls to finance if IV is rich.

options.thesis: Surprise pick — UNH +6.8% on raised 2026 guide ($19.50-20 EPS), pulling HUM +4.4% / CNC +3.5%. XLV +1.4% pre-mkt but UNH IV only 28% — realized vol will print higher on the day.
options.signal: Long UNH Jul 18 580 calls (~$3.50) into open as managed-care re-rate accelerates; take profit into the bell or if 590 holds as resistance.

takeaway: Split tape, not a break. Soft macro (208K claims, Philly Fed 41.4, retail +0.2%) underpins ES bid, but Iran 2nd-wave + chip complex sell-the-news (TSMC/WDC/STX/MU) + Netflix AMC + UAL oil drag = NQ -0.83% doing the work while YM +0.10% / XLV +1.4% (UNH, LLY-ATAI) cushion. VIX 16.2 is cheap into this stack. Trade: long QQQ/SPY put spreads into NFLX/close, sell TSLA vol, ride UNH/ATAI deal convexity, fade the chip unwind via SOXS or WDC puts. EOD tape hinges on Netflix — beat = green close, miss = vol expansion through 18.

headline: Risk-off open: fresh US airstrikes on Iran (3rd wave) spike WTI +3.4% and 10Y to 4.59% 7-week high, NQ -0.88% leads equities lower as oil/gold split, defense/energy bid — into a packed macro gauntlet (big-bank prints tomorrow, June CPI Tuesday).

sentiment.reason: Geopolitical shock (US 3rd-wave Iran strikes Sun) + oil +3.4% reignites stagflation tilt; 10Y to 4.59% = bond market pricing Fed-hold/Hawkish; NQ -0.88% with semis and AI complex leaking; only offsets are defense/energy bid and Friday's VIX crush to 15.8 (fragile).

movers.reason: Defense/Moon-stock bid on Iran escalation; space-defense complex re-rates on geopolitical tail.
Defense major bid as CENTCOM's 3rd-wave Iran strikes lift missile/air-defense order pipeline.
AI capex jitters + rising yields + Iran oil shock = double-whammy on long-duration semis; NQ -0.88% drag.

news.headline: CENTCOM completes 3rd-wave US strikes on Iran over the weekend; tanker incident + Hormuz traffic disruption reported
news.context: WTI +3.4% to $73.81, Brent back above $78; crude bid re-enters as Strait-of-Hormuz transit risk gets repriced. Defense/energy bid, risk-asset drag.

news.headline: 10Y yield jumps to 4.59% 7-week high on Iran shock + supply auction
news.context: Bond market pricing hawkish Fed: Iran oil shock is stagflationary, not growth-friendly. Gold -1.4% as real yields bid (counterintuitive haven fade).

news.headline: Mega-cap bank earnings kick off Tuesday: JPM, GS, WFC, Citi, BofA all pre-market
news.context: Trading-revenue beat expected on SpaceX IPO + Q2 vol; net-interest-margin the swing factor. JPM testing $343 record-high resistance into print.

news.headline: June CPI print Tuesday 8:30 ET — consensus +0.3% m/m core, focus on services inflation
news.context: Triple-stack catalyst: Iran shock + CPI + bank prints. Hot core = stagflation confirmation = hawkish-Fed reaction in bonds; soft = relief-rally window into JPM.

news.headline: NVDA -2% premkt; SKHYV continues +12% in when-issued trading post $26.5B SK Hynix IPO
news.context: Semis split-tape persists: AI compute (NVDA) leaks on yields + oil, but memory complex (SKHYV) holds the bid from Friday's 7x-oversubscribed debut.

options.thesis: NQ -0.88% with VIX only at 16.4 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized; dealer gamma flipping negative below 29,800. Iran shock + CPI/bank-print gauntlet = tail risk expanding fast.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (29,700/29,400) Monday; size small into CPI Tue 8:30 ET. VIX >18 = take profit; defined-risk for the macro triple-stack.

options.thesis: ES -0.29% with 10Y bid to 4.59% = real-yield headwind on duration-sensitive large caps. COR3M still compressed at ~9 — vol being sold into a stagflationary shock.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,550/7,450 put spreads July 17, fund with short 7,650 calls — risk-reversal for Iran/CPI/bank-print tail. Hedge-and-go for the gauntlet.

options.thesis: Lagging the tape into Q2 deliveries (next week); Iran oil shock + EV-demand concerns = continued underperformance. IV 55% with no fresh catalyst = vol gift, no directional edge.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~280) into CPI/bank prints; vol crush favors premium-sellers here, no edge in direction with macro binary ahead.

options.thesis: Iran 3rd-wave strikes + Hormuz tanker incident = the cleanest geopolitical crude play in weeks. XLE +1.85% pre-mkt, dealer call flow accelerating on Aug 60C strikes.
options.signal: Long XLE Aug 62 calls (~$1.80) as tactical oil-position; cheap convexity into the Hormuz-risk premium. Stop below WTI $70.

options.thesis: Surprise pick — defense major bid on Iran escalation; IV still cheap (~22%) vs realized on geopolitical setups. Missile/air-defense order pipeline re-rates fast on CENTCOM strikes.
options.signal: Long LMT Aug 580/620 call spread (~$4.50); cheap convexity into the defense re-rate, IV at 22 with geopolitical premium still building.

takeaway: Risk-off open, but the bigger story is the calendar. Iran 3rd-wave strikes + oil +3.4% + 10Y to 4.59% = stagflation tilt into a triple-stack macro gauntlet: bank prints Tue, CPI Tue, plus ongoing Hormuz risk. VIX at 16.4 is the cleanest 'fragile' signal — institutions not paying for tail into a binary week. Trade: long XLE/LMT into the defense-energy bid, hedge with QQQ/SPY put spreads into CPI/banks, sell TSLA vol, fade the calm.

asOf: Weekend digest — Sun, Jul 12 (Fri Jul 10 close)
headline: Friday closed risk-on near record highs: S&P 500 +0.80%, NQ led as semis bid (NVDA +4%, SK Hynix $26.5B US debut oversubscribed 7x) re-rated the AI-memory complex, AAPL re-rated on renewed Broadcom chip deal — BTC reclaimed $64K on oil weakness (WTI $72.05), but VIX crushed 13% to 15.84 leaves the tape fragile into Tuesday's CPI/Warsh/big-bank gauntlet.

sentiment.reason: Index green (S&P +0.80% Fri, +1.23% week) led by NVDA +4% and SK Hynix $26.5B IPO validation; VIX crushed 13% to 15.84 = institutional vol-selling confirming risk-on, but CRCL/DAL single-name dispersion and the Hormuz oil tail keep it from being a clean melt-up.

movers.reason: OCC greenlights national trust bank to hold USDC reserves — stablecoin infra re-rate ahead of July 18 GENIUS Act.
AI chip bid resumes on SK Hynix US debut validation + renewed AVGO/AAPL chip deal; closes $210.96.
Q2 record $19.8B revenue but fuel +77% YoY = highest in company history; EPS -26% YoY despite beat.

news.headline: SK Hynix prices $26.5B US ADR offering at $149 — 7x oversubscribed, biggest foreign IPO in history
news.context: Direct US trading closes 21% P/E gap to MU; trades 'when-issued' as SKHYV today, switching to SKHY Monday. Validates HBM/memory scarcity thesis and re-rates the semis complex.

news.headline: Circle (CRCL) +15% on OCC approval to launch national trust bank for USDC reserves
news.context: Stablecoin infra re-rate; gives USDC a federally regulated home ahead of the July 18 GENIUS Act implementation deadline.

news.headline: VIX crushed 13% to 15.84 as institutions sell vol into a packed macro gauntlet
news.context: Largest single-session VIX drop in 6 weeks; institutions running short-vol into Tuesday CPI + Warsh semi-annual + big-bank earnings — fragility rising even as tape looks calm.

news.headline: Apple renews Broadcom chip deal; AAPL bid resumes into iPhone 17 cycle
news.context: Multi-year AVGO chip commitment removes supply uncertainty for the iPhone 17 launch; pairs with SK Hynix validation to clear the AI-device overhang from last week.

news.headline: June CPI Tuesday, Fed Chair Warsh semi-annual, big-bank earnings kick off — full macro gauntlet
news.context: Triple-stack catalyst: CPI consensus +0.3% m/m core, Warsh's hawkish lean, JPM/WFC/Citi prints. Vol is being gifted into this calendar — fade the calm.

options.thesis: NQ green with VIX crushed to 15.84 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized into Tuesday's CPI + Warsh testimony. Dealers positioned short gamma, any hot print = violent squeeze.
options.signal: Buy QQQ weekly put spreads (29,700/29,400) into Monday close; VIX >18 = take profit. Defined-risk tail into the macro gauntlet.

options.thesis: VIX 15.8 with 10Y at 4.52% — vol is being sold at cycle lows into a packed catalyst calendar. COR3M still compressed at ~9.
options.signal: Long SPY 7,500/7,400 put spreads July 17, fund with short 7,700 calls — risk-reversal for the CPI tail.

options.thesis: Lagging the tape into Q2 deliveries; IV elevated (~55%) with no fresh catalyst. Straddle seller gets paid for waiting through the vol crush.
options.signal: Sell TSLA weekly straddle (~415) into Monday; vol gift, no edge in direction here.

options.thesis: +4% rip with IV still 50%+ post SK Hynix validation; dealer flow turning two-way as the AI-memory trade re-prices. Earnings-adjacent premium is rich.
options.signal: Sell NVDA Aug 220/240 call calendar against long stock — harvest IV into late-July print while keeping AI upside.

options.thesis: OCC trust bank is binary catalyst, IV expanding fast on $2.3B mkt cap add. 30D vol has 48-72h shelf life before GENIUS Act implementation on July 18.
options.signal: Sell CRCL weekly call spreads (95/110) into Friday close — IV crush setup, news-driven premium.

takeaway: Risk-on into the weekend close, but the VIX crush to 15.84 is the cleanest 'fragile' signal in weeks — institutions are selling vol at cycle lows into a triple-stack macro gauntlet (CPI Tue, Warsh, big-bank earnings). Trade: long NVDA into SK Hynix validation, fade CRCL IV into July 18 GENIUS Act, hedge with SPY put spreads. Don't trust the calm.

sentiment.reason: Index flat (ES -0.01%, NQ -0.43%, YM +0.24%) with single-name dispersion dominating: SK Hynix IPO re-rates semis complex, CRCL/DAL are binary catalysts, oil still bid on Hormuz tail — no clear risk-on/risk-off, just rotation.

news.context: Geopolitical tail live but not escalating; Brent +0.3% to $76.38, gold -0.4% to $4,103 as safe-haven premium bleeds. CPI Tuesday is the next real test.

news.headline: June CPI Tuesday, Fed Chair Warsh testifies, big-bank earnings kick off — full macro gauntlet
news.context: June CPI + Warsh semi-annual + JPM/WFC/Citi prints = triple-stack catalyst. Williams stays neutral; market pricing ~1 hike by year-end.

options.thesis: NQ -0.43% with VIX crushed to 16 — front-week IV mispriced vs realized into a CPI/Warsh triple-stack. Skew flat but tail risk is in the data, not the tape.
options.signal: Buy 0DTE QQQ 29,700/29,500 put spreads Tuesday if CPI hot; VIX >18 = take profit
